Syzygium aromaticum, commonly known as Cloves, is a spice and a plant that belongs to the family Myrtaceae. The health benefits of Cloves include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Cloves include eugenol, a phenolic compound responsible for its medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Cloves include teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to relieve toothache, coughs, and sore throats, as well as to reduce pain and inflammation.
